title: 以下、Kernel/FUGUEに活用できる？ Server: SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.11.9
summary_text: Minimum practical columns for a `keep / merge / replace / retire` inventory sheet: | Column | Why it is required for decisioning | |---|---| | `skill_name` | Unique row identity. | | `owner` | Accountability for fixes/migration. | | `primary_scope` | Clarifies intended boundary (capability vs orchestration). | | `usage_90d` | Distinguishes active vs dead skills. | | `system_leverage_score` (1-5) | Measures contribution to broader system, not local convenience. | | `functional_overlap` (none/partial/high) | Core signal for merge/retire. | | `hard_fail_flags` | Any blocker: policy bypass, implicit high-risk run, unpinned source, missing audit/replay/parity. | | `portability_score` (1-5) | Works across required model council/fallback paths or not. | | `operational_cost_score` (1-5) | Maintenance + runtime burden vs value. | | `candidate_target` | Merge target or replacement skill, if applicable. | | `decision` (`keep|merge|replace|retire`) | Final action field. | | `decision_rationale` | One-line justification for auditability. | If you want, I can give you a ready-to-paste CSV template with these columns plus scoring formulas.
